steps.title: Find the first intercept
steps.content:
For $f(x)=a(2.7)^{x+b}$, the $y$-intercept occurs at $x=0$:
$$
f(0)=a(2.7)^b
$$
That value requires evaluating the power, so it is not displayed directly as one constant or coefficient in equation I.

steps.title: Check the second equation
steps.content:
For $g(x)=a(2.7)^x+b$, substitute $0$ for the input:
$$
g(0)=a(2.7)^0+b=a+b
$$
The equation shows $a$ and $b$ separately, not the combined intercept value $a+b$, so equation II also does not display it directly.

steps.title: Choose neither equation
steps.content: Neither equation directly displays the $y$-coordinate of its $y$-intercept as a constant or coefficient. The answer is D.
